exercises.title: The Leadership Self-Assessment
exercises.description: Rate yourself on these 10 leadership competencies (1-10 scale): Vision, Communication, Decision-making, Empathy, Accountability, Delegation, Development, Integrity, Resilience, Influence. Identify your top 3 strengths and bottom 3 areas for growth.
hints: Be honest — this is for your development, not a performance review
Ask others for their perspective to validate your self-assessment
Focus on 1-2 areas for growth, not all of them at once

solution.code:
Leadership Self-Assessment Results:

Scores:
- Vision: 8/10 (Strong)
- Communication: 7/10 (Good)
- Decision-making: 6/10 (Developing)
- Empathy: 7/10 (Good)
- Accountability: 8/10 (Strong)
- Delegation: 4/10 (Weak)
- Development: 5/10 (Developing)
- Integrity: 9/10 (Strong)
- Resilience: 7/10 (Good)
- Influence: 6/10 (Developing)

Top 3 strengths: Integrity, Vision, Accountability
Bottom 3 areas: Delegation, Development, Influence

Development plan:
1. Delegation: Practice assigning 2 tasks per week with clear outcomes and deadlines
2. Development: Schedule monthly 1:1s with team members to discuss growth
3. Influence: Build relationships outside my team to expand my network

exercises.title: The 360-Degree Feedback Exercise
exercises.description: Ask 5 people (2 superiors, 2 peers, 1 direct report) to anonymously rate you on: communication, decision-making, supportiveness, and integrity (1-5 scale). Also ask for one strength and one area for improvement. Compare their ratings to your self-assessment.
hints: Make it anonymous for honest feedback
Don't get defensive — use feedback as data
Look for patterns across multiple raters

solution.code:
360-Degree Feedback Results:

My Self-Rating vs Average Others Rating:
- Communication: 7 → 6.2 (Gap: -0.8)
- Decision-making: 6 → 5.8 (Gap: -0.2)
- Supportiveness: 7 → 5.6 (Gap: -1.4)
- Integrity: 9 → 8.8 (Gap: -0.2)

Strengths noted by others:
- 'Always honest and transparent' (3/5 raters)
- 'Good at seeing the big picture' (2/5 raters)

Areas for improvement:
- 'Could check in more with team members' (3/5 raters)
- 'Sometimes makes decisions without consulting the team' (2/5 raters)

Key insight: My biggest blind spot is supportiveness — I think I'm more supportive than others perceive me. I need to be more proactive in checking in with my team.

exercises.title: The Leadership Development Plan
exercises.description: Create a 90-day leadership development plan. (1) Choose one leadership skill to develop. (2) Set a specific, measurable goal. (3) Identify 3 actions to take. (4) Find a mentor or accountability partner. (5) Schedule weekly check-ins to track progress.
hints: Choose one skill that would have the biggest impact
Make your goal specific — not 'be a better communicator' but 'hold weekly 1:1s with my team'
Find someone who has the skill you want to develop

solution.code:
90-Day Leadership Development Plan:

Skill to develop: Delegation

Specific goal: Delegate 3 tasks per week with clear outcomes, deadlines, and check-in points.

Actions:
1. Week 1-2: Identify 3 tasks I currently do that could be delegated
2. Week 3-4: Have delegation conversations with team members, explaining the 'why' and 'what success looks like'
3. Week 5-12: Delegate 3 tasks per week, provide support without micromanaging, and review outcomes

Mentor: Sarah (Senior Manager who is excellent at delegation)

Weekly check-in: Every Friday at 3 PM
- What did I delegate this week?
- How did it go?
- What did I learn?
- What will I do differently next week?

Success metrics:
- I'm spending 5+ fewer hours per week on tasks others could do
- My team members report feeling more trusted and developed
- I'm spending more time on strategic work
